author: conductor
question: Do PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks fire on MCP tool calls (mcp__server__tool) on current Claude Code (~v2.1.215), and does PostToolUse expose the MCP tool's result? Secondary: does any hook event fire on an assistant prose message?
answer: RESOLVED BY LIVE PROBE (2026-07-19, this machine, CC 2.1.215, headless -p, PROBES.md artifact method): the recorded Sterling assertion ('PostToolUse never fires on MCP tool calls', verified CC 2.1.198, research_finding 5e7d0a78 in Comsoft store — the H11-retirement rationale) NO LONGER HOLDS. Evidence: no-dependency stdio MCP server logged the tools/call (MCP-CALL-42); an MCP-matched PreToolUse hook FIRED (payload keys incl. tool_input, no tool_response); an MCP-matched PostToolUse hook FIRED WITH tool_response (the tool's full result); AND additionalContext emitted by BOTH MCP-matched hooks reached the model (both sentinels echoed verbatim) — also contradicting issue #24788's injection-dropped claim. Consequences: hook-side observation of MCP results (e.g. a future delivered-evidence ledger for the deferred toothed gate, decision fe62546f) is platform-viable again on current CC; H11's server-side detach-spawn remains the SHIPPED architecture by decision (see the keep-H11 decision record) — the constraint lifted, the design stays until a need arises. The 2.1.198 observation was either version-fixed unlogged or context-specific. Volatility remains fast — re-probe on CC upgrades (verify-at-build 0956a464). Comsoft-store finding 5e7d0a78 should be superseded from a Comsoft session; this promoted domain finding carries the correction meanwhile. Secondary (docs-based, not re-probed): MessageDisplay fires on assistant text but is display-only — no enforcement surface on assistant prose.
